words.def: in a different way from somebody/something
words.example: Boys and girls may behave differently.

words.def: the general position a person or thing moves or points towards
words.example: in the direction of something, They headed in the direction of the village.

words.def: one of a group of senior managers who run a company
if two people disagree or one person disagrees with another about something, they have a different opinion about it
to become impossible to see
to be the first person to become aware that a particular place or thing exists
words.example: Cook is credited with discovering Hawaii.

words.def: a conversation about somebody/something; the process of discussing somebody/something
words.example: I discovered something interesting during a family discussion.

words.def: a play for the theatre, television or radio
to make pictures, or a picture of something, with a pencil, pen or chalk (but not paint)
a picture made using a pencil or pen rather than paint
a series of images, events and feelings that happen in your mind while you are asleep
words.example: I had a really weird dream last night.

words.def: a journey in a car or other vehicle
an illegal substance that some people smoke, inject, etc. for the physical and mental effects it has
words.example: He does not smoke or take drugs.

words.def: not wet or sticky; without water or moisture
all through a period of time
near the beginning of a period of time, an event etc.
to get money for work that you do
words.example: earn (something), He earns about $40 000 a year.

words.def: the world; the planet that we live on
without problems or difficulty
words.example: The museum is easily accessible by car.

words.def: in or towards the east
a process of teaching, training and learning, especially in schools, colleges or universities, to improve knowledge and develop skills
a change that somebody/something causes in somebody/something else; a result
used after negative phrases to state that a feeling or situation is similar to one already mentioned
words.example: Pete can't go and I can't either.

words.def: connected with electricity; using, produced by or producing electricity
a very large animal with thick grey skin, large ears, two curved outer teeth called tusks and a long nose called a trunk. There are two types of elephant, the African and the Asian.
a way of sending messages and data to other people by means of computers connected together in a network
words.example: I will be on vacation for a week in November, with no access to email.

words.def: the last part of a story, film, etc.
words.example: His stories usually have a happy ending.

words.def: a person whose job involves designing and building engines, machines, roads, bridges, etc.
words.example: We worked with a team of scientists and engineers.

words.def: extremely large
to the necessary degree
words.example: I hadn't trained enough for the game.

words.def: to come or go into something
a mistake, especially one that causes problems or affects the result of something
more with one person, thing, etc. than with others, or more in particular circumstances than in others
words.example: Teenagers are very fashion conscious, especially girls.

words.def: a short piece of writing by a student as part of a course of study
words.example: I have to write an essay this weekend.

words.def: the unit of money of some countries of the European Union
words.example: I paid five euros for it.

words.def: used in negative sentences and questions, or sentences with if to mean ‘at any time’
used or happening every day or regularly; ordinary
words.example: Change is a part of everyday life in business.

words.def: in, to or at every place; all places
the facts, signs or objects that make you believe that something is true
words.example: evidence (of something), Researchers have found clear scientific evidence of a link between exposure to sun and skin cancer.

words.def: used to emphasize that something is correct in every way or in every detail
a formal written, spoken or practical test, especially at school or college, to see how much you know about a subject, or what you can do
extremely good
physical or mental activity that you do to stay healthy or become stronger
to be real; to be present in a place or situation
words.example: Does life exist on other planets?

words.def: to think or believe that something will happen or that somebody will do something
words.example: expect something, The company is expecting record sales this year.

words.def: to tell somebody about something in a way that makes it easy to understand
words.example: explain (something), He was trying to explain the difference between hip hop and rap.

words.def: a statement, fact, or situation that tells you why something happened; a reason given for something
words.example: The most likely explanation is that his plane was delayed.

words.def: to show or make known a feeling, an opinion, etc. by words, looks or actions
words.example: express something, Teachers have expressed concern about the changes.

words.def: a word or phrase
words.example: What's the meaning of the expression ‘on cloud nine’?

words.def: to a very high degree
words.example: It is extremely important to follow the directions exactly.

words.def: a thing that is known to be true, especially when it can be proved
words.example: fact about something, First, some basic facts about healthy eating.

words.def: a building or group of buildings where goods are made, mainly by machine
to not be successful in achieving something
words.example: Many diets fail because they are boring.

words.def: acceptable and appropriate in a particular situation
a person who admires somebody/something or enjoys watching or listening to somebody/something very much
extremely good; excellent
an area of land, and the buildings on it, used for growing crops and/or keeping animals
a popular style of clothes, hair, etc. at a particular time or place; the state of being popular
having too much flesh on it and weighing too much
being a woman or a girl
used with plural nouns and a plural verb to mean ‘a small number’, ‘some’
a type of literature that describes imaginary people and events, not real ones
an area of land in the country used for growing crops or keeping animals in, usually surrounded by a fence, etc.
words.example: We had to walk across a ploughed field.

words.def: a struggle against somebody/something using physical force
a series of moving pictures recorded with sound that tells a story, watched at a cinema or on a television or other device
words.example: Let's stay in and watch a film.

words.def: being or happening at the end of a series of events, actions, statements, etc.
words.example: his final act as party leader

words.def: after a long time, especially when there has been some difficulty or delay
words.example: The performance finally started half an hour late.

words.def: the last part or the end of something
words.example: It was a close finish as they had predicted.

words.def: before anyone or anything else; at the beginning
words.example: ‘Do you want a drink?’ ‘I'll finish my work first.’

words.def: a creature that lives in water, breathes through gills, and uses fins and a tail for swimming
the sport or business of catching fish
a journey made by air, especially in a plane
a small flying insect with two wings. There are many different types of fly and many of them spread disease.
words.example: A fly was buzzing against the window.

words.def: able to fly
to come or go after or behind somebody/something
used to show who is intended to have or use something or where something is intended to be put
in or from a country that is not your own
words.example: What foreign languages do you speak?

words.def: a large area of land that is thickly covered with trees
a tool with a handle and three or four sharp points (called prongs), used for picking up and eating food
words.example: to eat with a knife and fork

words.def: by good luck
words.example: I was late, but fortunately the meeting hadn't started.

words.def: recently produced or picked and not frozen, dried or preserved in tins or cans
a piece of electrical equipment in which food is kept cold so that it stays fresh
words.example: The kids tend to raid the fridge when they get home from school.

words.def: a small animal with smooth skin, that lives both on land and in water (= is an amphibian). Frogs have very long back legs for jumping, and no tail.
that you enjoy
words.example: She's really fun to be with.

words.def: objects that can be moved, such as tables, chairs and beds, that are put into a house or an office to make it suitable for living or working in
a room or building for showing works of art, especially to the public
any substance like air that is neither a solid nor a liquid, for example hydrogen or oxygen
words.example: Air is a mixture of gases.

words.def: a barrier like a door that is used to close an opening in a fence or a wall outside a building
a thing that you give to somebody, especially on a special occasion or to say thank you
a frame with a net into which players must kick or hit the ball in order to score a point
words.example: He headed the ball into an open goal (= one that had nobody defending it).

words.def: bright yellow in colour, like gold
words.example: The company name was spelled out in gold letters.

words.def: a game played over a large area of ground using sticks with a special shape to hit a small hard ball (a golf ball) into a series of 9 or 18 holes, using as few shots as possible
of high quality or an acceptable standard
